title: Evangelion RPG
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion RPG?             Quote Post by quatre1453 » Tue Jul 13, 2004 12:50 am is there any info on the eva rpg book? i forgot the publisher's name. thanks

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jul 13, 2004 10:41 am Guardians of Order Inc. will, in association with ADV, be releasing two Evangelion RPG guides this fall. They will detail the series and the director's cuts. If they will detail the movies is unclear. They will contain numerous color images, as well as character and episode guides, rule mechanics, roleplaying sheets, and more.

[]:             Quote Post by quatre1453 » Tue Jul 13, 2004 12:21 pm thanks

[]:             Quote Post by ChronoX » Wed Aug 25, 2004 7:30 pm there will be eva rpg's released this fall?!?!?!?!?!? will they be released in america or just in Japan

[]:             Quote Post by Karma Burn » Sun Sep 05, 2004 11:27 am An eva RPG? Guardians of Order? Oh wait, now I remember...GoO makes the Big Eyes, Small Mouths RPG (BESM for short). Basically, it's an anime bastardization of Dungeons&Dragons. There's a Trigun source book for it. I was very dissapointed with that. I've got a feeling I'm going to be dissapointed with the Eva source book as well. Take it from a guy who plays paper and pencil RPGs, BESMs is a waste of time. If you want to play RPGs stick with the big names... Wizards of the Coast: Dungeons and Dragons, d20 Modern (which can be easily adapted to an giant robot RPG), and Starwars White Wolf: World of Darkness (Vampire: The Masquerade, Werewolf: The Apocalypse, Mage: The Ascension...nothing to do with eva or giant robots but very cool none the less)

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Tue Nov 02, 2004 5:19 pm Yeah the Trigun D20 book was more like an artbook then anything, about 3/4ths of it was a "summary" of the story, but I would buy the Eva one anyway, not to RPG with but to look at.

[]:             Quote Post by Oblivious » Fri Nov 19, 2004 9:08 am I wish there was one for the Ayanami Raising project... in english.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Nov 19, 2004 10:55 am I'm being sent review copies at the end of November, I'll let you guys know what these books are about when I get them.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Dec 02, 2004 12:14 pm The BESM Evangelion RPG supplements are in the process of being approved, and with the holiday season its unlikely they'll be out any time before January. I may have comp copies to review in January, at which time I'll fill people in as to what these books are all about.

[]:             Quote Post by anatrok » Fri Dec 03, 2004 4:58 pm Eva Monkey, you lucky punk. I want those books!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Dec 03, 2004 5:05 pm If I took all the hours I spent working on this site, posting on forums, and researching, and worked in a restaurant bussing tables instead, I could probably afford a Renault Alpine. No joke. I've probably put that many hours into this site. Occassionally I get something free, however it pales compared to what I could be making investing my time into a job. I'm a bit dissapointed, I was interested to see how an Evangelion RPG could work out. Now I'll have to wait till January. Stupid Christmas. :x

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jul 28, 2005 1:06 am In case you didn't read it on the main page, I would like to reiterate that the Evangelion BESM books have been cancelled. You can read more at the link below: http://www.evamonkey.com/2005/07/eva-besm-cancelled.php

[]:             Quote Post by cyraka » Thu Sep 22, 2005 3:56 pm I have heard of games based on Evangelion but I haven't been able to find them...

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Thu Sep 22, 2005 7:03 pm The reason you havent been able to find them in stores is because you have to impiort them becasue the games dont have a wide audience in the western world

[]:             Quote Post by cyraka » Mon Sep 26, 2005 12:46 pm Timesplitter 01 wrote: The reason you havent been able to find them in stores is because you have to impiort them becasue the games dont have a wide audience in the western world

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Sep 26, 2005 12:59 pm The NGE RPG is quoted in vol8 of the manga as Â¥1900 (i.e. ~$19US); and it's in Japanese. ISBN 4-04-714513-0. Or you could use some imagination and BESM/Hero/GURPS (or even d20 *ack* *thhpt*).
